notes: KNOWN FAILING (turn_end + teardown phases only) — the live recording shows the headless `aider --message` process exits ~2.6s after the turn_start working flip (state_transition working at +1010ms, process_exited at +3.6s), which is BEFORE the daemon's documented working→ready settle: IdleFlush synthesizes turn_done after ~1.5s of quiescence but is clamped to the SessionDetector's 5s working-refresh poll, so the working→ready edge can't fire within the process's ~2.6s post-turn lifetime. No state_transition back to ready is ever emitted, so turn_end has no anchor and teardown cascades. The load-bearing phases DO match (session_birth, pid_bind, turn_start = 3/5). INHERENT headless fast-exit observability gap (the assessed observes:partial carve-out — a latency floor, NOT a correctness gap, NOT a daemon regression, NOT a spec error, NOT a rebase): the committed recipe is a headless `prompt` cell with no script to extend, and --re-record mode forbids re-authoring to an interactive driver. Same shape as the pi/turn-aborted-by-error precedent (headless process exits before the daemon's observation window). Re-record and clear this flag if the daemon gains sub-process-lifetime working→ready binding for FilesUnderCWD adapters (e.g. a turn_done flush on process_exited), or if the recipe is upgraded to an interactive aider session that outlives the 5s poll. Original spec rationale follows. Smallest possible round-trip: one user prompt, one assistant reply, no tool use, asserting the ready→working→ready arc with TotalTokens > 0 after the turn. Recorded with the HEADLESS aider driver (`aider --message <prompt>` in a fresh per-run git cwd): aider stores its transcript as .aider.chat.history.md in the cwd (FilesUnderCWD), so the daemon keys the session by proc-<PID> rather than a transcript UUID — there is no separate rollout session phase, the whole arc stays on the one proc-<PID> row. The daemon observes that session born ready on the first scanner tick, the working flip when the `#### ` user-prompt line lands, the working→ready settle when aider's turn-end is inferred (the `> Tokens:` line closes the model call and IdleFlush synthesizes turn_done after ~1.5s of quiescence, clamped to the SessionDetector's 5s working-refresh poll), then process_exited at headless exit. turn_end anchors to turn_start (not session_birth) so the matcher picks the post-turn ready, not the birth ready. The working→ready edge lands with ~5s effective latency (the assessed observes:partial carve-out) — that's a timing delay, not a correctness gap, so the arc is still asserted. The TotalTokens > 0 assertion the scenario validates isn't expressible in the phase DSL today (no metric form in the invariant DSL); it's covered by the aider parser unit test TestParser_BaselineHello_FullTurn.

text: Session appears in ready within 1s of launch
Assistant reply completes → back to ready; state never entered waiting
Headless aider exits after the single turn — process_exited on the session
